new_classes.definition_en: A material entity that serves as the input to a distillation process for the production of grape spirits; either grape wine (the fermented juice of fresh grapes) or grape marc (the solid residue remaining after pressing grapes).

A distillation process whose input is grape wine or grape marc, yielding spirits classified under CN subheading 2208 20; includes primary distillation (yielding wine distillate / raw distillate of wine) and further distillation to produce finished spirits such as Cognac or Grappa.

new_classes.label_en: Spirit obtained by distilling grape wine or grape marc (CN 2208 20)
new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage obtained by distillation of grape wine or grape marc, including wine distillate (raw distillate of wine) obtained by primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine; it does not bear the qualities of neutral ethyl alcohol nor of a fully-finished spirit in the sense that wine distillate is also covered.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the three main distinguishing criteria of CN 2208 20: (1) the product class is a spirituous beverage (subclass of SpiritousBeverage); (2) it is produced by a distillation process (GrapeDistillationProcess); (3) that distillation process takes grape wine or grape marc as its feedstock (GrapeDistillateFeedstock). The inclusion of wine distillate / raw distillate (primary distillation product after alcoholic fermentation) is captured in the class definition. A small coverage gap remains: the legal text explicitly states the product does NOT have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol nor of a ready-for-consumption spirit (the 'not neutral alcohol' criterion) — this is expressed in the definition text but cannot be fully modelled as a clean OWL complement restriction without a NeutralEthylAlcohol class present in the current TBox; the complement restriction referencing a non-existent IRI was therefore kept only as a semantic comment in the definition. Additionally, the legal note references HS Explanatory Notes (external cross-reference) which carry no product-discriminating OWL content. Coverage is estimated at 0.75 due to the inability to formally axiomatise the 'not neutral ethyl alcohol' exclusion within the current TBox.
